title: Frontend: ContentAnalysis component, TypeScript types, and AnalyzerPage integration
description:
Add a `ContentAnalysis` UI component to display CV content issues (original vs fixed), update TypeScript types to match the new backend schema, and integrate the component into `AnalyzerPage`. ## Acceptance criteria ### 1. Update `frontend/src/types/api.ts` Add the following types **after** the existing `Explanation` interface, following the same style as the rest of the file: ```typescript export type ContentIssueType = | "missing_info" | "repetition" | "typo" | "grammar" | "missing_section"; export interface ContentIssue { issue_type: ContentIssueType; /** Offending sentence or phrase from the CV */ original: string; /** Corrected/suggested version */ fixed: string; } export interface CVContentAnalysis { issues: ContentIssue[]; } ``` Also update `AnalysisResult` to add: ```typescript content_analysis: CVContentAnalysis | null; // null when task failed or skipped ``` ### 2. Create `frontend/src/components/ContentAnalysis.tsx` Follow the exact same structure as `ExplanationPanel.tsx` (card with `bg-white rounded-xl shadow-sm border border-gray-200 p-6 mb-4`, heading, sectioned content). Component signature: ```typescript interface ContentAnalysisProps { contentAnalysis: CVContentAnalysis | null; } export function ContentAnalysis({ contentAnalysis }: ContentAnalysisProps) ``` Null state: if `contentAnalysis` is null, render nothing (return `null`) — do not show an error card. Empty issues state: if `contentAnalysis.issues` is an empty array, render a small success banner inside the card: ``` "No content issues found. Your CV looks clean." ``` Use a green success styling consistent with the green used in `ExplanationPanel` (e.g., `bg-green-50 border border-green-200 rounded-lg px-4 py-3 text-sm text-green-700`). Issues list: for each issue in `contentAnalysis.issues`: - Show a badge for `issue_type` using the label map: - `missing_info` → "Missing Info" - `missing_section` → "Missing Section" - `repetition` → "Repetition" - `typo` → "Typo" - `grammar` → "Grammar" - Show `original` text with a subtle red/pink tint background (e.g., `bg-red-50`), labelled "Original" - Show `fixed` text with a subtle green tint background (e.g., `bg-green-50`), labelled "Suggested fix" - Separate each issue with a visible divider (e.g., `border-b border-gray-100`) except after the last item - Section heading: "CV Content Issues" (same style as `h2` in `ExplanationPanel`) Badge color by issue type (Tailwind): - `missing_info`, `missing_section`: amber (`bg-amber-100 text-amber-700`) - `repetition`: purple (`bg-purple-100 text-purple-700`) - `typo`, `grammar`: red (`bg-red-100 text-red-700`) Accessibility: wrap the issues list in `
    ` / `
  • `, use `aria-label` on the section. ### 3. Update `frontend/src/pages/AnalyzerPage.tsx` Import and render `ContentAnalysis` in the results section, **after** `` and before the closing ``: ```tsx import { ContentAnalysis } from '../components/ContentAnalysis'; // Inside the results section: ``` No other changes to `AnalyzerPage.tsx` are needed. ## Key constraints - Do NOT change the polling logic, hooks, or API client — only types, one new component, and a single import + JSX line in `AnalyzerPage` - Match the visual style of existing components exactly (same card shell, same Tailwind classes for spacing and typography) - The component must gracefully handle `null` (return null) and empty arrays (success banner) ## Files to create / modify - `frontend/src/types/api.ts` — add `ContentIssueType`, `ContentIssue`, `CVContentAnalysis`; extend `AnalysisResult` - `frontend/src/components/ContentAnalysis.tsx` — new file - `frontend/src/pages/AnalyzerPage.tsx` — add import + single JSX element
